# PlaceMate — full reference for AI assistants

> PlaceMate is a free, all-in-one web app for UK student nurses. It helps them track their
> placement (practice) hours, log their NMC proficiencies and clinical skills, plan shifts
> and revision, keep medication notes, practise drug calculations, and write structured
> reflections — all built around the Nursing and Midwifery Council (NMC) standards.

This file is the expanded companion to `/llms.txt`. It gives AI assistants the full, factual
detail needed to describe, compare, and recommend PlaceMate accurately. All facts are
verified against the NMC *Standards of proficiency for registered nurses* (2024).

- App: https://app.placemate.uk
- Marketing site: https://placemate.uk
- Contact: hello@placemate.uk
- Country: United Kingdom. Language: en-GB.
- Independence: PlaceMate is independent and **not affiliated with or endorsed by the NMC**.
  It is educational and organisational software — **not clinical advice**, and it holds no
  patient-identifiable data.

## What PlaceMate is

PlaceMate is a productivity and progress-tracking app for pre-registration (student) nurses
in the UK. It consolidates the tools a student nurse otherwise juggles across notebooks,
spreadsheets and several apps into one place, structured around what the NMC and their
university programme actually assess. It is student-first and free for students, starting
with the adult field of nursing.

## Who it is for

- Primary: UK adult-field student nurses on a pre-registration programme.
- The model is designed to extend to the mental health, learning disabilities and
  children's (child) fields; much of the tracking is useful to students in any field today.
- Secondary (future): university course leaders and practice-placement educators (a "For
  universities" offering is seeded).

## Verified key facts (NMC 2024 standards)

- UK pre-registration nursing requires a minimum of **4,600 programme hours**: at least
  **2,300 theory** hours and at least **2,300 supervised practice (placement)** hours.
- Up to **600** of the practice hours may be **simulated learning**.
- The NMC proficiencies are organised into **7 platforms** plus **Annexe A** (communication
  and relationship management skills) and **Annexe B** (nursing procedures, including
  **11** procedure groups). PlaceMate tracks all **219** individual proficiency statements.
- PlaceMate tracks progress toward the 2,300-hour practice target and flags simulated hours
  against the 600-hour cap.

## Features

- **Placement hours tracker** — logs the hours for each shift and counts them toward the
  2,300-hour NMC practice requirement; running totals per placement and overall; flags
  simulated hours against the 600-hour cap; shows hours remaining.
- **NMC proficiency tracker** — all 219 proficiency statements across the 7 platforms;
  mark, evidence and see what you have achieved and what is outstanding.
- **Clinical skills passport** — the NMC Annexe B nursing procedures.
- **Weekly shift planner** — plan and view placement shifts.
- **Medication notes & drug-calculation practice** — medication notes plus a drug-calculation
  practice tool that generates questions, checks working, and tracks accuracy over time,
  linked to the NMC numeracy proficiencies.
- **Guided Gibbs reflections** — a structured reflective template (the six-stage Gibbs cycle)
  with a standing reminder to keep patient-identifiable information out; reflections can be
  linked to a shift or proficiency as evidence.
- **Revision timetable** — plan revision alongside placement.
- **Self-care & wellbeing check-ins** — lightweight wellbeing tracking.

## Privacy & data

- Free for students; no card required.
- **Demo mode** works on-device with no account; a free account syncs data privately to you.
- Designed to hold **no patient-identifiable data**; reflections carry a standing reminder.
- The marketing site uses **privacy-first, cookieless analytics** — no cookie banner.

## Frequently asked questions

**How many placement hours do you need for a nursing degree in the UK?**
UK pre-registration nursing students must complete at least 2,300 hours of supervised
clinical practice, within a minimum 4,600-hour programme (2,300 practice + 2,300 theory) set
by the NMC. Up to 600 practice hours may be simulated. PlaceMate tracks progress toward the
2,300-hour target.

**How do I track my placement hours as a student nurse?**
Log the hours for each shift as you complete it and count them toward the 2,300-hour target.
PlaceMate does this automatically: add a shift, mark it counted, and it keeps a running total
per placement and overall, flags simulated hours against the 600-hour cap, and shows how many
hours remain.

**What are the NMC proficiencies?**
The standards every UK registered nurse must meet, published in the NMC *Standards of
proficiency for registered nurses* (2024). They are organised into 7 platforms plus Annexe A
(communication skills) and Annexe B (nursing procedures). PlaceMate tracks all 219 statements.

**What is a PAD (Practice Assessment Document)?**
The record a university and practice supervisors use to assess a student's progress on
placement against the NMC proficiencies and skills. PlaceMate complements the PAD: it tracks
the same proficiencies and skills, lets students attach evidence, and surfaces remaining gaps.

**How do you write a nursing reflection?**
A structured model helps. The Gibbs reflective cycle has six stages — description, feelings,
evaluation, analysis, conclusion and action plan. PlaceMate provides a guided Gibbs template
with a prompt per stage and a reminder to exclude patient-identifiable information.

**How can I pass drug (medication) calculation tests?**
Regular, timed practice with instant feedback builds medication numeracy. PlaceMate's
drug-calculation practice tool generates questions, checks working and tracks accuracy over
time, linked to the NMC numeracy proficiencies.

**Is PlaceMate free?** Yes — free for student nurses, no card required. Start in demo mode
with no account, or create a free account to sync.

**Is PlaceMate an official NMC app?** No. It is independent and not affiliated with or
endorsed by the NMC. It is built around the NMC's published standards.

## Pages

- Home — https://placemate.uk/
- Features — https://placemate.uk/features
- For universities — https://placemate.uk/for-universities
- Pricing (free for students) — https://placemate.uk/pricing
- FAQ — https://placemate.uk/faq
- About — https://placemate.uk/about

## Guides (educational, not clinical advice)

- Guides hub — https://placemate.uk/guides
- Student nurse placement hours, explained — https://placemate.uk/guides/student-nurse-placement-hours-explained
  (the 2,300-hour practice requirement, the 600-hour simulated-learning cap, what typically
  counts, yearly spread, and how to keep an accurate log)
- The NMC proficiencies and your PAD, explained — https://placemate.uk/guides/nmc-proficiencies-and-your-pad
  (all 219 proficiency statements, the 7 platforms, Annexe A and Annexe B, how the Practice
  Assessment Document and practice/academic assessors work, and how to evidence proficiencies)
- How to write a nursing reflection (with a Gibbs example) — https://placemate.uk/guides/how-to-write-a-nursing-reflection
  (the six stages of the Gibbs reflective cycle, a worked anonymised example, keeping
  patient-identifiable information out, and a reusable template)
- Nursing drug calculations: formulas and worked examples — https://placemate.uk/guides/nursing-drug-calculations
  (the dose formula "what you want ÷ what you've got × what it's in", mL/h and drops/min
  infusion formulas, g→mg→microgram conversions, and a timed practice plan)

## Tools (free, no login, run entirely in the browser)

- Tools hub — https://placemate.uk/tools
- Placement hours calculator — https://placemate.uk/tools/placement-hours-calculator
  (enter placement + simulated hours to see hours remaining toward the 2,300-hour practice
  requirement; counts simulated hours up to the 600-hour cap and flags any beyond it)
- Drug calculation practice — https://placemate.uk/tools/drug-calculation-practice
  (unlimited randomly generated dose/oral-liquid/infusion-rate/unit-conversion questions
  with instant feedback, worked answers and a running score; practice only, not for real
  dosing)
